name: Soy milk (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (unfortified plain soymilk; ~ per 100 mL, density ~1.02); mM computed as (mg/100g x 10)/MW assuming density 1.0

components.name: total protein (soy protein)
components.source: USDA FDC soymilk unfortified original & vanilla (SR NDB 16223); unsweetened 3.55 g
components.evidence: Glycinin/beta-conglycinin; dominant organic N source. Free amino acids are a small fraction; profile below is total protein-derived

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC soymilk unsweetened total sugars 0.56 g (predominantly sucrose); Valliyodan 2015 doi:10.1155/2015/757649 (sucrose is the dominant free soybean sugar)
components.evidence: Chief fermentable free sugar of plain soymilk. NOTE: sweetened/original products carry 3-6 g/100g added sugars

components.name: stachyose
components.source: Soymilk oligosaccharide study (initial stachyose ~330 mg/100 mL before fermentation; Omogbai et al.); soybean 1.34 g/100g (Nielsen 2024 PMC11202694)
components.evidence: Raffinose-family alpha-galactoside; the defining soy oligosaccharide, MISSING from current record. Fermentable only by alpha-galactosidase-positive microbes

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Soymilk RFO range 30-274 mg/100 mL; soybean 0.34 g/100g (PMC11202694)
components.evidence: Raffinose-family alpha-galactoside, MISSING from current record

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: soybean free glucose 0.07-0.43 g/100g (PMC11202694), heavily diluted in soymilk
components.evidence: Present at trace (<0.05 g/100g); not separately quantified in plain soymilk

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: soybean free fructose 0.08-0.21 g/100g (PMC11202694), heavily diluted
components.evidence: Present at trace (<0.05 g/100g); not separately quantified in plain soymilk

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: regular soymilk 2.61 g/kg (Sprague-Dawley low-phytate soymilk study, ScienceDirect S1756464623001950)
components.evidence: Dominant organic phosphate/acid of soy; cation chelator (limits free Ca/Fe/Zn availability). MISSING from current record

components.name: citric acid
components.source: soybean organic-acid profile: citric among predominant acids (Zhang 2020 doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1); soy-beverage HPLC (PubMed 25466130)
components.evidence: One of the two predominant soy organic acids; present in plain soymilk but not quantified per 100g in accessible sources. MISSING from current record

components.name: malic acid
components.source: malic is the main organic acid of (vegetable) soybean, > citric (Zhang 2020 doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1)
components.evidence: Co-dominant soy organic acid; present but unquantified per 100g. MISSING from current record

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Oxalate & Phytate of Soy Foods (Iowa State, Massey)
components.evidence: Low in soymilk; present, unquantified in accessible copy

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant PUFA of soy oil; MISSING from current record

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: n-3 PUFA; MISSING from current record

components.name: oleic acid (18:1)
components.source: USDA FDC soymilk unfortified (total MUFA 0.40 g, ~all oleic)
components.evidence: Chief MUFA; MISSING from current record

components.name: palmitic + stearic acid (saturated)
components.source: USDA FDC soymilk unfortified (total SFA 0.20 g; palmitic dominant)
components.evidence: Saturated fraction; palmitic ~0.16 g, stearic ~0.04 g

components.name: L-Glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant protein amino acid (incl. glutamine)

components.name: L-Serine
components.evidence: protein-derived. Replaces the erroneous D-Serine in current record

components.name: L-Methionine
components.evidence: protein-derived; sulfur-limiting amino acid of soy

components.name: L-Cysteine
components.source: soy protein Cys ~1.3% of protein (FAO soy AA scoring); USDA lists cystine 0 for this diluted item
components.evidence: Present but low/unquantified in this USDA record; MISSING from current record

components.name: Phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: Partly bound as phytate; predominant anion

components.name: Calcium
components.evidence: Native value. NOTE: calcium-fortified soymilks reach ~100-120 mg/100g

components.name: Iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the duplicate Fe entries (Iron / Iron Fe3+ / Fe2+ mitochondria) in current record

components.name: Iodine
components.source: current record; native soy iodine very low

components.name: Nickel
components.source: current record; legumes accumulate Ni (urease cofactor)

components.name: Pantothenic acid (B5)
components.evidence: B-vitamin; amount was MISSING from current record

components.name: Folate (B9)
components.source: USDA FDC soymilk unfortified (DFE; unsweetened 20 ug)

components.name: Beta-Carotene
components.evidence: trace provitamin-A carotenoid; native retinol activity ~0

components.name: L-Ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: absent natively; present only if fortified/flavored

remove.name: Caffeine
remove.reason: not a soybean constituent; FooDB cross-contamination/misassignment. Soymilk contains no caffeine (only chocolate/tea-flavored variants would)

remove.name: Theobromine
remove.reason: not a soybean constituent; methylxanthine from cacao/tea, FooDB noise, biologically implausible in plain soymilk

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin of Apiaceae/citrus, not soy; FooDB predicted/misassigned, implausible in soy matrix

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: animal-form vitamin A; native unfortified soymilk retinol activity ~0. Present only in vitamin-A-fortified products

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin (B12)
remove.reason: native soymilk B12 = 0 (plants do not synthesize B12); appears only via fortification. Not a native growth nutrient of the food

remove.name: Vitamin D / Ergocalciferol
remove.reason: native soymilk vitamin D = 0; present only in D-fortified products. Ergocalciferol in the uncovered list is a fortification/predicted artifact, not native

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: stereochemistry artifact; soy protein serine is L-Serine. Replaced by L-Serine in components

keep_uncovered: (genuine soy isoflavones/lignans/coumestans - real phytochemicals, no BiGG metabolite)
sources: USDA FoodData Central - Soymilk, original and vanilla, unfortified (SR Legacy NDB 16223)
Nielsen et al. 2024, Regional Variability in Sugar/AA Content of U.S. Soybeans, PMC11202694
Valliyodan et al. 2015, Chromatography Research Int'l, doi:10.1155/2015/757649 (soybean sugars)
Zhang et al. 2020, Food Production Processing and Nutrition, doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 (soybean organic acids)
PubMed 25466130 - soluble sugars, organic acids, minerals in milk/soy-juice beverages
ScienceDirect S1756464623001950 - low-phytate soymilk phytic acid 2.61 g/kg
Omogbai et al. - stachyose in soymilk yogurt (initial ~330 mg/100 mL)
Massey - Oxalate and Phytate of Soy Foods (Iowa State)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on plain soymilk are sucrose (chief fermentable free sugar) plus the raffinose-family oligosaccharides stachyose and raffinose (fermentable only by alpha-galactosidase-positive organisms), with soy protein and its amino acids (Glu/Asp-rich) as the dominant organic-nitrogen source; organic acids (malic, citric, phytic) are minor. Profile is for UNFORTIFIED plain soymilk - calcium (25 vs ~120 mg), vitamin B12, vitamin D and vitamin A are essentially zero natively and appear only via commercial fortification (flagged in remove[]). Curation added the previously missing soy oligosaccharides, phytic/organic acids, fatty acids, and amino acids Val/Phe/Cys; corrected D-Serine to L-Serine and consolidated duplicate iron entries; removed caffeine/theobromine/oxypeucedanin as FooDB misassignments implausible in soy.
